entries.definition:
كتاب اللاّم

لوح - لوذ

(ليل) اللام والياء واللام كلمة، وهي اللَّيل: خِلافُ النهار. يقال ليلةٌ ولَيْلات. وأمَّا اللَّيالي .

لَوَّوْا lawwaw [v. II, trans.] to twist several times, to cause to be
crooked *(63:5) لَوَّوْا رُءُوسَهُمْ they resisted, they showed disdain [lit.
they twisted their heads].

لَيّ layy [v. n. used adverbially] twisting *(4:46) لَيًّا بِأَلْسِنَتِهِمْ
distorting with their tongues, distorting the words.
entries.definition_english:
لَوَّوْا lawwaw [v. II, trans.] to twist several times, to cause to be
crooked *(63:5) لَوَّوْا رُءُوسَهُمْ they resisted, they showed disdain [lit.
they twisted their heads].

لَيّ layy [v. n. used adverbially] twisting *(4:46) لَيًّا بِأَلْسِنَتِهِمْ
distorting with their tongues, distorting the words.

entries.definition:
l–y–l night, night-time, one night, to enter night-time. Of this
root, four forms occur 92 times in the Qur’an: اللَّيْل ʾal-layl 74
times; لَيْل layl five times; لَيْلَة laylatun nine times and لَيَالِي layālī

four times.

ٱلَّيْل ʾal-layl [n.] 1 night as opposed to day (41:37) وَمِنْ آيَاتِهِ اللَّيْلُ
وَالنَّهَارُ وَالشَّمْسُ وَالْقَمَرُ and of His signs are the night and the day, the
sun and the moon 2 the darkness of night (6:76) فَلَمَّا جَنَّ عَلَيْهِ اللَّيْلُ رَأَىٰ
كَوْكَبًا when the night grew dark above him he saw a star 3 span of
the night, night-time (73:2) قُمِ اللَّيْلَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا keep vigil throughout the
night, all but a small part of it.

لَيْل layl [n. used adverbially] by night (17:1) سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي أَسْرَىٰ بِعَبْدِهِ
لَيْلًا glory be to Him who made His servant travel by night; *(71:5)
إِنِّي دَعَوْتُ قَوْمِي لَيْلًا وَنَهَارًا I called my people [night and day]
continuously, all the time; * الليل name of Sura 92, Meccan sura,
so-named because of the reference in verse 1 to the ‘Night’.

لَيْلَة laylatun [fem. n., pl. لَيَالِي layālī] a night (44:3) إِنَّا أَنزَلْنَاهُ فِي لَيْلَةٍ
مُّبَارَكَةٍ We have sent it down on a blessed night!; *(97:1) لَيْلَةُ الْقَدْر
(see ٱلْقَدْر ʾal-qadr); *(89:2) لَيَالٍ عَشْر layālin ʿashr [ten nights].
Opinions differ as to the meaning of this: the last ten days of the
month of رَمَضَان Ramaḍān (in which لَيْلَةُ الْقَدْر laylatu’l-qadr (q.v.),
may fall, the first ten days of the month of ذُو الْحِجَّة dhū’l-Hijjati
during which the pilgrimage takes place, or the first ten days of
the month of الْمُحَرَّم ʾal-Muḥarram, which is the first month in the
Islamic calendar.
